subject: Accounting
title: Short-Term vs. Long-Term Debt
description: A 60-second lesson on Short-Term vs. Long-Term Debt.

hook.text: A company owes $2 million. But when it's due changes everything.

buildup.text: Short-term debt (current liabilities) must be paid within twelve months.

discovery.text: Long-term debt stretches over years—mortgages, bonds, multi-year loans.

twist.text: Companies sometimes reclassify long-term debt as short-term when it nears maturity—a red flag.

climax.text: A healthy balance sheet matches the timing of debt to the assets it finances.

punchline.text: It's not just how much you owe. It's when.

quiz.question: What qualifies as a current liability?
